action.reasoning: Observation: Best mae_60min is 3.7138 from iteration 12. We have ~21 minutes and 17 iterations left. Analysis: Instead of risky long training from scratch, the highest-ROI strategy is: (1) compute a per-step/per-sensor additive bias correction on the validation set using the existing best model, (2) fine-tune a second model starting from the best checkpoint for ~15 epochs with a lower LR and more aggressive late-horizon loss weights, (3) update predict.py to ensemble both models, apply bias correction, and use test-time augmentation (TTA). Fine-tuning is fast (~8-10 min) because we resume trained weights. The ensemble + bias + TTA should improve the 60-min horizon without risking the current best checkpoint.
action.command: python compute_bias.py && python train.py --resume checkpoints/best.pt --epochs 15 --batch_size 128 --lr 0.001 --ckpt_name best2.pt --seed 42
